title: Reviewer rotation + load balancing
context: As the project scales (scale-001), relying on a single reviewer for each role risks a bottleneck and reviewer burnout. M3 requires a reviewer rotation and load-balancing scheme to be established so that no one reviewer is required for every batch and substitutes are identified. This depends on ≥2 qualified reviewers (reviewers-002) being available and requires a published rotation policy.
objective: Define and document a reviewer rotation and load-balancing policy: specify how review assignments are distributed across the qualified reviewer pool; define the minimum frequency at which each reviewer rotates onto a batch; define a substitute/backup assignment process for when a primary reviewer is unavailable; specify how the rotation is recorded (e.g., in each review.yaml); and update the reviewer roster (from reviewers-002) to reflect rotation assignments.
acceptanceCriteria: Rotation policy document specifies how domain-aware reviewer and bilingual reviewer assignments are distributed across the qualified reviewer pool so no single reviewer handles every batch
Defines minimum rotation frequency (e.g., no reviewer handles more than N consecutive batches without rotation) and how exceptions are approved by the maintainer
Defines a substitute/backup assignment process: who approves, how the substitute's qualification is confirmed, and how the substitution is recorded
Specifies where rotation assignments are recorded (e.g., a field in review.yaml or a rotation log file)
Reviewer roster updated to reflect current rotation assignments; policy consistent with the independence and COI rules in reviewers-001
resources: multilingual-museum-labels-reviewers-002 (qualified reviewer roster — must be in place)
output: docs/reviewer-rotation.md — rotation and load-balancing policy; updated docs/reviewer-roster.md with rotation assignments
